title: Database Access on LDAP
text: Database Access on LDAP Logon Path Specify the logon path to the LDAP server using the a path-to-connector assignment in the [PATHS] section: $LDP=LDP: Host { + Port } : Searchbase | User | Password Host —network node name for the host, for example www.traders.com . Port —port number on Host on which the LDAP Server process is listening; the default LDAP port is 389 for TCP and 636 for TLS. Searchbase —location in the LDAP tree where the search starts. It can contain spaces, but does not allow anonymous access. For more information, see LDAP Data Structure . Note: Due to the nature of LDAP, the LDAP connector tries to store updated entries at this level even when they have been retrieved from a different level. To circumvent this behavior, it is possible to use entity redirecting to store entries at the correct level. User —entry in the LDAP directory that represents an authorized user. Password —password of User to log on to Host . Host (including Port ), User , and Password may each be replaced by a question mark (?) or completely omitted. If the missing information is required, a Logon dialog box for the network is displayed. If neither user name nor password is specifed, Uniface attempts an anonymous logon. Identifying the User The Searchbase and User must combine to form a unique reference to the LDAP entry that stores the user’s details. For example, for user eddie , if Searchbase is o=MyCompany , the User must be the distinguished name (DN) of this entry: [paths] $LDP = LDP:myhost:o=MyCompany|uid=eddie,ou=People,ou=MyCompany|eddie Copy However, if the Searchbase specifies part of the LDAP entry, and the addbase connector option is set on , the User can be just uid=eddie : [PATHS] $LDP = LDP:myhost:ou=People,o=MyCompany|uid=eddie|eddie [DRIVER_SETTINGS] USYS$LDP_PARAMS addbase:on Copy Example: Anonymous Logon In the following example, www.traders.com is the server acting as host, and o=accounting.com is the organization used as the LDAP search base. $LDP LDP:www.traders.com+389:o=accounting.com| | Copy Example: Logon Form Displayed Specifying question marks for the User and Password forces a logon form to be displayed: $LDP = LDP:www.traders.com+389:o=accounting.com|?|? Copy The end user can specify the user name and password but is not allowed to enter a server or search base setting. Example: User DN In the following path setting, the User is the complete entry holding J Smith’s user information: $LDP2 LDP:host.com+390:c=nl|uid=jsmith,ou=billing,o=accounting ltd|password Copy Open/Close Support The open and close Proc instructions are supported. The open instruction opens the relevant path and attempts to log on to the LDAP server. The close instruction logs off from the LDAP server, shutting down that path. Related Links LDAP Connector The Uniface LDAP connector enables you to connect with LDAP servers, including stand-alone LDAP servers and X.500 Directory System Agents with an LDAP gateway installed. Path-to-Connector Assignments A path-to-connector assignment describes how to access a specific resource via the appropriate network or database connector. It defines the path name, and assigns it to a configuration that specifies the connector to use, the name and location of the resource, and the credentials required to log on to it. Paths are defined in the [PATHS] section of the application assignment file. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
